title: dicerandra frutescens var. modesta
intro: Dicerandra frutescens var. modesta, also known as the 'Rough Mint' or ' Coastal Blue Flagroot', is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is rich in essential oils, particularly camphor, and has been known to exhibit health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Dicerandra frutescens var. modesta can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy and topical applications.
